content: Drug discovery is the process of identifying potential therapeutic compounds. It begins with target identification—finding a biological molecule (protein, gene) involved in disease pathogenesis. Hit-to-lead involves screening thousands of compounds to find 'hits' that interact with the target, then optimizing them into 'leads' with better potency and selectivity. Preclinical testing evaluates safety, pharmacokinetics, and efficacy in vitro and in animal models before human trials. The process typically takes 3-6 years with high attrition rates.
